questions.question: What is the amplitude of the function y = 3sin(x)?
questions.explanation: The amplitude of y = a·sin(x) is the absolute value of the coefficient a. Here, |3| = 3.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A = 50°, angle B = 70°, and side a = 10. Use the Law of Sines to find side b to the nearest whole number.
questions.explanation: By Law of Sines: a/sin(A) = b/sin(B). So 10/sin(50°) = b/sin(70°). Therefore b = 10·sin(70°)/sin(50°) ≈ 10(0.94)/0.77 ≈ 11.

questions.question: What is the period of the function y = cos(2x)?
questions.explanation: For y = cos(bx), the period is 2π/b. Here b = 2, so period = 2π/2 = π.

questions.question: In triangle ABC, a = 5, b = 7, and angle C = 60°. Use the Law of Cosines to find side c.
questions.explanation: Law of Cosines: c² = a² + b² – 2ab·cos(C). So c² = 25 + 49 – 2(5)(7)cos(60°) = 74 – 70(0.5) = 39. Thus c = √39.

questions.question: At what value of x in the interval [0, 2π] does y = sin(x) first reach its maximum?
questions.explanation: The sine function reaches its maximum value of 1 at x = π/2 within one complete period [0, 2π].

questions.question: In triangle PQR, angle P = 35°, angle Q = 85°, and side p = 8. Find angle R and then use the Law of Sines to find side q to the nearest whole number.
